taxonomy.title: Plug Taxonomy
taxonomy.description: A governed intervention classification layer for identifying which structural plug class is required to restore trust, continuity, clarity, recovery, or alignment inside a digital conversion path.
taxonomy.purpose: To translate diagnosed leak classes into structured intervention logic without collapsing the system into generic software recommendations or decorative solution language.
taxonomy.methodology_note: A plug class is not a product listing, template bundle, or software category. It is a structural intervention class that describes what must be restored for the path to recover integrity.

decision_framework.primary_recommendation_rule: The primary plug class should correspond to the primary diagnosed leak class unless a stronger upstream structural blocker overrides intervention order.
decision_framework.secondary_recommendation_rule: A secondary plug class may be recommended when a second leak class remains materially meaningful and operationally adjacent.
decision_framework.intervention_order_rule: Plug classes should be ordered by structural dependency, not by visual attractiveness or implementation convenience.
decision_framework.non_product_rule: Plug taxonomy does not identify commercial tools directly in v1. It identifies intervention classes that can later map to systems, patterns, or implementation layers.
decision_framework.non_claim_rule: Plug recommendation does not guarantee uplift. It guides structural remediation priority.

plug_classes.title: Trust Infrastructure
plug_classes.description: Intervention layers that restore confidence, legitimacy, reassurance, and proof at decision-critical moments.
plug_classes.structural_definition: Trust Infrastructure is the plug class used when the path asks for commitment before it has built enough confidence to support that commitment. It reinforces the system's ability to feel credible, safe, legitimate, and decision-worthy.
plug_classes.intervention_purpose: To reduce trust-based hesitation and strengthen confidence at conversion boundaries.

deployment_priority.reason: When trust is underbuilt, increased traffic or improved visuals rarely solve the real problem.

strategic_effect.primary: Transforms fragile decision moments into confidence-supported commitment points.
strategic_effect.secondary: Reduces late-stage leakage caused by credibility weakness rather than offer irrelevance.

methodology_notes: Trust Infrastructure is not just design polish.
It should be deployed where decision confidence is required, not sprayed across the path indiscriminately.
This plug class becomes especially important when interest exists but completion stalls.

output_templates.headline: Recommended primary plug class: Trust Infrastructure
output_templates.summary_sentence: The path requires stronger confidence architecture before it can reliably convert intent into commitment.
output_templates.next_step_sentence: Reinforce proof, legitimacy, reassurance, and risk-reduction at decision-critical points.

plug_classes.title: Flow Continuity Layer
plug_classes.description: Intervention layers that preserve movement, reduce transition failure, and maintain continuity from entry to action.
plug_classes.structural_definition: Flow Continuity Layer is the plug class used when intent is not being carried cleanly through the path. It restores momentum by reducing structural interruption, transition decay, and discontinuity between stages.
plug_classes.intervention_purpose: To protect movement integrity and preserve conversion momentum across the path.

deployment_priority.reason: If the path cannot carry momentum, even strong demand and strong trust become structurally fragile.

strategic_effect.primary: Reduces leakage caused by momentum collapse before completion.
strategic_effect.secondary: Improves the system's ability to convert intent without demanding repeated re-orientation from the user.

methodology_notes: Flow Continuity is not merely about speed or aesthetics.
It should be deployed where movement logic is structurally interrupted.
This plug class often supports, but does not replace, friction reduction.

output_templates.headline: Recommended primary plug class: Flow Continuity Layer
output_templates.summary_sentence: The path requires stronger continuity architecture to carry intent from entry through action.
output_templates.next_step_sentence: Stabilize transitions, preserve momentum, and reduce interruption points across the journey.

plug_classes.title: Friction Reduction Layer
plug_classes.description: Intervention layers that reduce avoidable effort, process burden, and completion drag without degrading structural clarity.
plug_classes.structural_definition: Friction Reduction Layer is the plug class used when the path demands more effort than necessary. It reduces operational burden, cognitive strain, and procedural heaviness that suppress movement or completion.
plug_classes.intervention_purpose: To remove unnecessary burden so the system can convert with less effort expenditure from the user.

deployment_priority.reason: Friction reduction becomes critical once continuity is weak enough to suppress intent through process burden.

strategic_effect.primary: Reduces conversion loss caused by process heaviness rather than offer weakness.
strategic_effect.secondary: Increases the path's ability to preserve intent under procedural load.

methodology_notes: Friction Reduction should not create under-explained simplicity that harms decision clarity.
The objective is not minimalism for its own sake, but burden reduction with structural integrity preserved.
This plug class is most useful where effort is clearly disproportionate to user readiness.

output_templates.headline: Recommended primary plug class: Friction Reduction Layer
output_templates.summary_sentence: The path requires targeted burden reduction to prevent effort from overpowering intent.
output_templates.next_step_sentence: Reduce unnecessary complexity, compress avoidable effort, and restore movement efficiency.

plug_classes.title: Decision Guidance Layer
plug_classes.description: Intervention layers that improve interpretability, value clarity, choice comprehension, and next-step certainty.
plug_classes.structural_definition: Decision Guidance Layer is the plug class used when the path does not provide enough interpretive support at the moment of choice. It clarifies what is being offered, why it matters, what happens next, and how the commitment should be understood.
plug_classes.intervention_purpose: To reduce ambiguity and support confident decision-making.
activation_signals: users must interpret too much on their own

deployment_priority.reason: If the user cannot confidently interpret the offer, the rest of the path loses conversion power.

strategic_effect.primary: Transforms interpretive hesitation into actionable clarity.
strategic_effect.secondary: Improves the conversion path's ability to support choice without overloading the user.

methodology_notes: Decision Guidance is not just copy expansion.
Its purpose is to support interpretation, not simply add more text.
It becomes especially important when users are interested but structurally under-guided.

output_templates.headline: Recommended primary plug class: Decision Guidance Layer
output_templates.summary_sentence: The path requires stronger interpretive guidance so the user can understand and choose with confidence.
output_templates.next_step_sentence: Clarify the value, the commitment, and the next action before attempting more aggressive persuasion.

plug_classes.title: Recovery Systems Layer
plug_classes.description: Intervention layers that restore lost momentum after hesitation, interruption, or abandonment.
plug_classes.structural_definition: Recovery Systems Layer is the plug class used when the path lacks meaningful re-entry architecture. It builds the continuation logic required to reclaim interrupted intent rather than surrender it permanently.
plug_classes.intervention_purpose: To convert interruption into recoverable movement instead of terminal loss.
activation_signals: re-entry prompts are absent or ineffective
the system depends too heavily on first-pass completion

deployment_priority.reason: Recovery systems matter most once the primary path has at least basic structural viability.

strategic_effect.primary: Recovers intent that would otherwise be lost after interruption.
strategic_effect.secondary: Expands the path from first-pass conversion logic into governed continuation logic.

methodology_notes: Recovery Systems are not generic reminders alone.
They should be structurally connected to the diagnosed interruption pattern.
This plug class becomes more valuable as funnel interruptions become more common but still recoverable.

output_templates.headline: Recommended primary plug class: Recovery Systems Layer
output_templates.summary_sentence: The path requires stronger continuation and re-entry logic after hesitation or abandonment.
output_templates.next_step_sentence: Build structured recovery paths that reclaim interrupted intent instead of letting it disappear.

plug_classes.title: Alignment Reinforcement Layer
plug_classes.description: Intervention layers that restore continuity between promise, context, intent, and path action logic.
plug_classes.structural_definition: Alignment Reinforcement Layer is the plug class used when the path does not faithfully continue the expectation that created entry. It reinforces continuity between pre-click promise, on-page narrative, and action-path logic so the system remains structurally coherent from arrival to decision.
plug_classes.intervention_purpose: To eliminate expectation drift and restore promise-path continuity.
activation_signals: entry intent is not being structurally served
the path asks for something different than what was implied
the user arrives with one expectation but meets another path logic
conversion weakens because continuity of intent was broken at arrival

deployment_priority.reason: Misalignment can contaminate the entire path before local optimization layers have a chance to work.

strategic_effect.primary: Restores continuity between why the user entered and what the path actually asks them to do.
strategic_effect.secondary: Improves relevance retention and reduces leakage caused by expectation violation.

methodology_notes: Alignment Reinforcement is not generic messaging refinement alone.
It must repair continuity at the structural level, not just improve wording.
This plug class is especially important when traffic quality appears strong but arrival relevance decays immediately.

output_templates.headline: Recommended primary plug class: Alignment Reinforcement Layer
output_templates.summary_sentence: The path requires stronger continuity between the entry promise and the action logic that follows.
output_templates.next_step_sentence: Restore promise-path alignment before refining lower-path conversion details.
